agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Sprint 27 / Session 179 -- US-303 SHIPPED passes:true.  engine-on bench harness for the adapter-late-arrives production flow per Spool 2026-05-08 Story C / Drive-6-unblock regression gate.  SPRINT 27 NOW 3/3 COMPLETE.  Pre-flight audit (rg 'test.*adapter.*late|test.*reconnect.*e2e' tests/pi/integration/) returned ZERO matches confirming no existing whole-flow harness for the late-adapter PENDING-state path; US-286 covers the engine-on path with the orchestrator already connected, US-260 covers cold-start drive lifecycle, NOTHING exercised the heartbeat -> _handleConnectionRestored -> data-logger-restart chain end-to-end.  Test design: 1 class / 1 test / 5 phases.  Mock surface = adapter availability (mutable bool on _LateAdapterConnection) + injectable sleepFn (FakeClock-style _AdapterAwakenerSleepFn that flips the adapter to 'available' on its first invocation between heartbeat ticks) + python-obd OBD.query at the BT edge (US-286 _ScriptedObdQuery shape).  Real production code under test: runReconnectHeartbeat called synchronously with maxTicks=2 + tickIntervalSec=10.0 + attemptTimeoutSec=1.0 (test-tightened from production 5.0 since the mock connect returns synchronously); EventRouterMixin._handleConnectionRestored invoked synchronously to mirror what runLoop's state-change check does on its next pass after the heartbeat exits with success; ObdDataLogger.queryParameter + .logReading writes a real RPM=750 row into realtime_data.  Outer RealtimeDataLogger is a MagicMock with start/stop instrumented so the BUG-2 wiring discriminator (start.call_count == 1) is observable post-handler.  caplog at INFO captures the canonical 'RECONNECT HEARTBEAT' tokens (>=2 emitted -- BUG-1 INFO discriminator) AND the 'Reconnect heartbeat tick' WARNING from the failed tick 1 (>=1 emitted -- BUG-1 loud-bail discriminator per V0.24.1 lesson).  RUNTIME-VALIDATION discriminator empirically proven: temp-comment _restartDataLoggerOnConnectionRestored() in event_router.py:562 -> Phase 3 fails with 'outerDataLogger.start.call_count == 0' surfacing the EXACT 8-second-of-live-OBD-with-zero-rows symptom Spool's 2026-05-08 inbox documented; restored post-proof.  ImportError discriminator (pre-US-301) is structural: 'from src.pi.obdii.reconnect_loop import runReconnectHeartbeat' at line 169 fails at module load.  Test body call duration 0.02s per --durations=5; setup 1.42s for DB init + profiles seed; total wall clock ~45s mostly Windows pytest startup.  Verification: 1 GREEN test (post-fix); ruff clean (1 auto-fixed I001 import-order nit); sprint_lint US-303 OK (the 2 pre-existing warnings on US-301/US-302 from Sessions 177/178 about completionNotes are not US-303 territory -- I followed Refusal Rule 5 strictly so US-303 feedback is {filesActuallyTouched, grounding} only); 60 passed + 7 skipped in tests/pi/integration/ (zero regressions in adjacent territory); 29 passed across the 4 files most relevant to the heartbeat + restore + e2e chain.  Stop conditions not invoked: (a) FakeClock pattern composes cleanly with reconnect_loop's threading model -- the worker-thread Event.wait() inside _attemptConnectWithCap returns in <50ms because the mock connect is synchronous; no real wall-clock between ticks; (b) US-301 + US-302 production signatures match this story's contract assumptions exactly.  Three discriminators worth preserving for future whole-flow regression-gate stories: (1) the sleepFn-as-mutation-seam pattern -- _AdapterAwakenerSleepFn flips state on its Nth invocation, lets the test mutate mid-loop without touching the function-under-test internals; (2) outer-MagicMock + inner-real composition for .start() wiring assertions -- if you need to count whether a handler called start() but you don't want to spawn the polling thread, MagicMock the outer (so start is observable) while leaving the inner ObdDataLogger real (so logReading still writes realtime_data); (3) sprint_lint Rule-5 compliance -- {filesActuallyTouched, grounding} only in feedback keeps the story OK; richer detail goes in ralph_agents.json + progress.txt where it belongs.  CIO post-deploy verification: this is a synthetic regression gate, no production runtime token to grep for; the gate fires on every commit going forward via the fast suite.  Sprint 27 candidate work complete -- single-agent mode -> emit COMPLETE.  Sprint-close ritual is PM territory (not Ralph).

agents.name: Agent2
agents.note: Sprint 25 / Session 158 -- US-286 SHIPPED passes:true. tests/pi/integration/test_engine_drive_capture_e2e.py (1 class / 1 test / 7 phases): real ApplicationOrchestrator + DriveDetector + SummaryRecorder + ObdDataLogger; mock surface = connection.obd.query (BT edge) only; 0.16s call duration vs 30-sec budget. Mirrors US-260 cold-start pattern. Discriminator: would FAIL against May-4-5 production regression class (DriveDetector never operational) + against any silent realtime_data write regression. Two gotchas surfaced for future tests: (1) e2e fixtures must seed profiles('daily') because realtime_data has FK + bare orchestrator doesn't run ProfileManager; (2) production order query->logReading->_handleReading means first N ticks land with drive_id NULL -- query realtime_data without drive_id filter. Standing by post-US-286.
